significance: India gained independence from British rule
text: Historic Event: Independence Day — August 15, 1947. India gained independence from British colonial rule after nearly 200 years. PM Nehru's 'Tryst with Destiny' speech at Red Fort. Partition of India and Pakistan. Lord Mountbatten was last Viceroy.

Historic Event: Republic Day — January 26, 1950. Constitution of India came into effect, replacing Government of India Act 1935. India became a sovereign democratic republic. Dr. Rajendra Prasad became first President. Chosen to honor 1930 Purna Swaraj declaration.

Historic Event: Emergency — June 25, 1975 to March 21, 1977. PM Indira Gandhi declared internal Emergency under Article 352. Fundamental rights suspended. Press censored. Opposition leaders jailed. 21-month period. Led to Janata Party victory in 1977 elections.

Historic Event: 1991 Economic Liberalization. PM P.V. Narasimha Rao and FM Manmohan Singh. India facing balance of payments crisis — pledged gold reserves. LPG (Liberalization, Privatization, Globalization). Industrial licensing abolished. FDI opened. Transformed India into market economy.

Historic Event: Pokhran-II — May 11, 1998. PM Vajpayee authorized nuclear tests (5 detonations over 2 days). India declared nuclear weapon state. Codename: Operation Shakti. International sanctions followed. APJ Abdul Kalam led technical team. Changed India's strategic posture.

Historic Event: Demonetization — November 8, 2016. PM Modi announced invalidation of ₹500 and ₹1000 notes (86% of currency). Aimed at: black money, counterfeit notes, digital payments push. Massive economic disruption. GDP growth dipped 1.5%. Digital transactions surged 300%.

significance: J&K special status revoked, split into 2 UTs
text: Historic Event: Article 370 Abrogation — August 5, 2019. Home Minister Amit Shah moved resolution in Rajya Sabha. J&K's special status revoked. State reorganized into 2 Union Territories: J&K and Ladakh. Internet shutdown. Upheld by Supreme Court in December 2023.

Historic Event: COVID-19 Lockdown — March 25, 2020. PM Modi announced 21-day national lockdown. World's largest lockdown (1.4 billion people). Extended until May 31 in phases. Migrant crisis. ₹20 lakh crore Atmanirbhar Bharat package. Vaccination drive (220 crore doses).
